prompt:
Reference Use:
1 person reference image attached to this instruction

Main Text:
Based on 1 person reference image attached to this instruction, create a simplified character sheet image for both sharing and video reference in a 1:1 square format.

The goal is to fix the appearance of one protagonist for a 15-second 3D daily comedy video titled "The protagonist, thinking they overslept for work, rushes to get ready and bursts out to leave, only to realize it's Sunday" and make the sheet easy to view for sharing.
This image is a character fixation sheet, not a storyboard. Do not include chronological scene progression, camera flow, or event reproduction.

The expression is high-quality stylized 3D.
Avoid cheap TV CG styles, monotonous NPC styles, or textures that look too toy-like. Emphasize a natural sense of depth on par with theatrical short 3D animation, approachable modeling, easy-to-read expressions, and an organized layout.

Reflect the facial features, hairstyle, hair color, sense of age, body type, atmosphere, and basic outfit based on the person in the reference image. Make the persona of the reference image immediately recognizable and reconfigure it as a shared character sheet.

The background is a clean studio background in white to very light gray.
Include short English headings and numbers, and refine it to a level of completion suitable for public sharing. Do not include long descriptions, logos, watermarks, or excessive decoration.

[Fixed Overall Layout]
Fix the canvas in a 3-tier horizontal configuration.

The top tier is approximately 50% of the total height. Arrange 01 FRONT, 02 SIDE, 03 BACK, and 04 PAJAMAS as four sections of the same height in a horizontal row from left to right.

The middle tier is approximately 22% of the total height. Fix 05 EXPRESSIONS as a horizontal area and arrange five facial expressions of the same size in a horizontal row from left to right.

The bottom tier is approximately 28% of the total height. Fix 06 FACE / DETAILS as a horizontal area.

Do not change the height of each tier, the order of sections, or the horizontal/vertical ratios based on the person or hairstyle. Do not move 05 to the rightmost column. Do not let 06 intrude into the top or middle tiers.

01 FRONT
Frontal full body of the protagonist's basic outfit. Reflect the outfit, color scheme, silhouette, shoes, bags, and accessories from the reference image.

02 SIDE
Side full body in the same basic outfit. Ensure the hairstyle, body type, outfit silhouette, and shoes are visible.

03 BACK
Rear full body in the same basic outfit. Ensure they are recognizable as the same person even from behind.

04 PAJAMAS
Full body in pajamas for waking up, showing the same person. Display in a front or slightly diagonal full body view. Pajamas should consist of a soft sleepwear material, separated top and bottom with sleeves and loose long pants. Make them barefoot. Clearly change the primary color, exposure, length, and silhouette from the basic outfit so they are immediately recognizable as sleepwear. Avoid camisoles, lingerie styles, short pants, skin-tight fits, or sleepwear with high skin exposure. Keep facial features, hairstyle, and body type consistent with 01-03.

05 EXPRESSIONS
Using the same basic outfit as 01-03, arrange the following 5 expressions in a horizontal row with the same size, height, and face scale:
1. NEUTRAL
2. SLEEPY / DROWSY
3. SHOCKED
4. PANICKED / HURRIED
5. DRAINED / SUNDAY REALIZATION
Display each expression within the same range from the top of the head to the upper shoulders. Do not enlarge or reduce specific faces based on the person or hairstyle. Do not change the expression images to pajamas.

06 FACE / DETAILS
Fix the horizontal area at the bottom of the canvas into a 60% left and 40% right split. Do not change this ratio.
The left 60% contains 3 facial views in the same basic outfit as 01-03: FRONT CLOSE-UP, 3/4 CLOSE-UP, SIDE CLOSE-UP. Arrange the 3 sections in a horizontal row with the same width and height. Display all three from the top of the head to the upper shoulders at the same scale, unifying face size, eye level, and margins. Do not widen sections for hair length or face shape.
The right 40% contains 4 sections of equal size fixed in a 2-column by 2-row layout:
Top left: EYES, Top right: LIPS, Bottom left: HAIR / BANGS, Bottom right: EAR / ACCESSORY. Do not change the number of sections, placement, or ratio on the right. Do not create 3-column configurations, 6 sections, vertical sections, merged sections, or additional sections. Do not display duplicate eyes, mouths, hair, or ears. Do not add unspecified details like collars, outfits, fabric, or chest area.

The number of people is only one protagonist. Configure everything as differences in direction, outfit, expression, and detail for the same person; do not include other people, groups, or twins.

Do not include scene elements like alarm clocks, calendars, beds, rooms, entrances, or offices. Overall, finish it as an organized, high-quality 3D character sheet that is easy to show for sharing and easy to use as a video reference later.
